description: Directory Traversal (Path Traversal) exploits insufficient validation of file-path input, allowing an attacker to read arbitrary files outside the intended web root using sequences such as '../'. Sensitive files such as /etc/passwd, application configs, and private keys may be exposed.

mitre_attack.mapping_note: T1083 applies only when traversal is used to discover files or directories. T1190 describes exploitation of the public-facing vulnerable endpoint.

cvss_methodology.note: CVSS measures vulnerability severity. It does not prescribe SOC response deadlines.
cvss_methodology.migration_note: CVSS v4.0 is current, but its metrics and scoring are not one-to-one with v3.1. Existing scenario vectors remain v3.1 until individually re-assessed.

response_framework.name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3
response_framework.note: Response events implement Detect, Respond, Recover, and continuous-improvement outcomes. Numeric exercise thresholds are local policy, not NIST requirements.

timing_model.note: The 900-second floor prevents zero-length and division-by-zero windows at CVSS 10.0. These values are simulation policy and must be calibrated with organizational risk and service objectives.

incident_response_rules.description: Alert triage started within the difficulty-adjusted MTTA threshold
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_investigation_started occurs after mtta_threshold_sec or is absent

incident_response_rules.description: Incident assessed and confirmed or formally dismissed
incident_response_rules.trigger: neither incident_confirmed nor dismissal_approved is present

incident_response_rules.description: Relevant evidence preserved before destructive containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: incident_confirmed is present but evidence_preserved is absent or occurs after containment_initiated
containment_initiated is absent or occurs after ttc_max_sec

incident_response_rules.description: Root cause or malicious artifact eradicated after containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: containment_succeeded is present but eradication_completed is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Recovery validated before normal operation resumed
incident_response_rules.trigger: eradication_completed is present but recovery_validated is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Lessons learned and improvement actions recorded
incident_response_rules.trigger: recovery_validated is present but lessons_learned_recorded is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Medium-or-higher alerts are not denied without documented approval
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_denied is present for severity medium or higher without a preceding dismissal_approved event

scoring.ttc_factor: 1 when containment succeeds by ttc_expected; linear decay to 0 at ttc_max; 0 when containment is absent or later than ttc_max

response_difficulty_bonus.condition: awarded only when containment_succeeded is present
response_difficulty_bonus.zero_delay_policy: A zero-second investigation delay uses a one-second denominator and therefore receives the capped bonus.

stock_rules.description: Common web attack; its patterns include directory-traversal sequences
Multiple common web attacks from the same source IP

custom_rules.description: Application path-resolution telemetry shows the canonical path outside the allowed root

wazuh.note: Custom entries are dataset detection specifications and must be implemented and tested in the deployed Wazuh manager before use. Stock rule descriptions and levels match the linked upstream ruleset.

scenarios.label: Unauthenticated Plaintext Path Traversal — High Confidentiality Impact
scenarios.description: Attacker submits a GET request with an unencoded '../' sequence to a file-download endpoint, reading /etc/passwd and application config files. No authentication required. Integrity and availability unaffected.

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AC_L: No special conditions; '../' sequences are not filtered
component_rationale.C_H: Arbitrary file read; secrets and credentials exposed

detection.rationale: Plaintext '../' traversal sequences appear directly in web access logs and require no decoding. Detection uses stock Wazuh rule 31104 (Plain path-traversal sequence in a web request); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Plain path-traversal sequence in a web request

event_log.detail: Wazuh stock rule 31104 level 6 fired: Plain path-traversal sequence in a web request.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 300s, within 600s low threshold. Access log confirmed multiple traversal attempts; /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, and .env file successfully read by attacker.
Analyst-2 confirmed live exploitation: replayed request returned /etc/passwd contents. Application credentials in .env file exposed to attacker.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 applied path canonicalisation patch: chroot jail enforced on file-download handler; allowed file extensions restricted to .pdf and .csv.
Re-test returns 403 for traversal payloads. Exposed credentials rotated. .env file relocated outside web root. TTC_actual=7800s < TTC_expected=9000s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

scenarios.label: URL-Encoded Path Traversal — Alert Incorrectly Dismissed
scenarios.description: Attacker uses URL-encoded traversal sequences (%2e%2e%2f) to bypass a naive blacklist filter. The Wazuh alert is dismissed as a false positive, and the attacker continues to exfiltrate sensitive files unimpeded.

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AC_H: URL-encoding bypass requires specific evasion knowledge; WAF may partially block
component_rationale.C_H: Sensitive files readable once encoding bypass succeeds

detection.rationale: URL-encoded sequences require URL-decoding before pattern matching; standard WAF rules may not decode before alerting, leading to higher false-positive rates and analyst uncertainty. Detection uses stock Wazuh rule 31104 (URL-decoded path-traversal sequence); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

event_log.detail: Wazuh stock rule 31104 level 6 fired: URL-decoded path-traversal sequence.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 500s, within 750s medium threshold. Analyst inspected raw request but did not URL-decode the parameter before analysis.
The analyst denied the alert without documented approval. This triggers DIR-R09; all other missing response actions are evaluated independently.

scenarios.label: Authenticated Low-Privilege Traversal with Integrity Side-Effect
scenarios.description: Authenticated low-privilege user exploits a document-preview endpoint to traverse outside the upload directory, reading sensitive config files and a limited set of writable temp files. Scope unchanged.

attack_context.rationale: Attacker requires a valid low-privilege user account to access the document-preview feature.

component_rationale.AC_L: No special conditions; path parameter is concatenated without validation
component_rationale.PR_L: Low-privilege user session required to access preview endpoint
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to web process and accessible filesystem paths
component_rationale.C_H: Arbitrary file read within web-process filesystem scope
component_rationale.I_L: Limited write: attacker can overwrite temp files accessible to web process

detection.rationale: Authenticated endpoint traversal requires correlating file-access events in auditd with the authenticated user's session ID; lower traffic volume aids correlation but WAF rules are less sensitive for auth users. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100303 (Resolved path escaped the configured document root); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Resolved path escaped the configured document root

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100303 level 8 fired: Resolved path escaped the configured document root.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 700s, within 750s medium threshold. Session log correlated: doc_user accessed /etc/nginx/nginx.conf, /home/appuser/.ssh/id_rsa, and /tmp/app_cache/session.json.
Analyst-2 confirmed traversal: replay showed nginx.conf and SSH private key accessible. Temp file overwrite confirmed via inotify log showing doc_user wrote to /tmp/app_cache/.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 applied chroot restriction to preview endpoint — document root restricted to /uploads/; doc_user account suspended; SSH key pair regenerated.
Re-test returns 403 for all traversal payloads. Preview endpoint now serves only from /uploads/. TTC_actual=10200s < TTC_expected=10440s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

references.title: FIRST CVSS v4.0 Specification (current migration target)

dataset_metadata.source_policy: Use upstream standards and vendor rulesets; label project-specific detection logic as custom.
dataset_metadata.cvss_profile: Validated CVSS v3.1 compatibility profile; migrate through a separate expert review rather than automatic vector conversion.
